class.name: Tentacle
class.brief_description: PBD-based tentacle: a chain of mass particles with a spline-skinned mesh.
class.description: Owns one [PBDSolver] (a chain of particles connected by distance + bending constraints) and one [MeshInstance3D] driven by a unique [ShaderMaterial] that reads spline data from a per-tick [code]RGBA32F[/code] data texture. Anchored at the node's transform; the chain hangs from the anchor and is shaped by gravity, optional pose targets, and contact with the world. [b]Authoring split:[/b] this node owns the [i]physics character[/i] (chain length, segment count, stiffness, friction, collision). Behavioral knobs (wave / thrust / coil / attractor) live on a sibling [code]TentacleBehavior[/code] node so a single tentacle can swap moods at runtime. [b]Collision:[/b] each particle issues a sphere shape query against the physics server every tick (slice 4D), so the chain reacts uniformly to [StaticBody3D], [RigidBody3D], [AnimatableBody3D] and [PhysicalBone3D] (ragdoll bones). Friction reciprocals are routed back to dynamic bodies via [code]PhysicsServer3D.body_apply_impulse[/code] so a heavy chain dragging across a ragdoll bone pulls the bone in the drag direction.

method.name: clear_anchor_override
method.description: Restore the default anchor (the chain's particle 0 follows this node's [code]global_transform[/code] each tick).

method.name: clear_pose_targets
method.description: Drop all soft per-particle pose pulls.

method.name: clear_target
method.description: Drop the single tip target-pull, if any.

method.name: get_anchor_state
method.description: Snapshot of the active anchor: [code]{ particle_index: int, world_xform: Transform3D }[/code]. [code]particle_index = -1[/code] when no anchor is set.

method.name: get_environment_contacts_snapshot
method.description: One entry per particle (size = [member particle_count]) describing the latest tick's collision probe result. Each entry is a [Dictionary] with keys [code]particle_index[/code], [code]query_origin[/code], [code]hit[/code] (bool), [code]hit_point[/code], [code]hit_normal[/code], [code]hit_object_id[/code], [code]hit_linear_velocity[/code], [code]friction_applied[/code]. Consumed by the debug overlay; safe to read every frame (returns by copy).

method.name: get_particle_inv_masses
method.description: Per-particle inverse mass. 0 = pinned (anchor / rigid base); 1.0 = unit mass; values in between scale with the [code]TentacleBehavior.mass_from_girth[/code] helper.

method.name: get_particle_positions
method.description: Per-particle world positions, snapshot copy. Index 0 is the anchor.

method.name: get_rest_girth_texture
method.description: The 256-bin rest-girth profile (FORMAT_RF) read by the vertex shader to scale radius along arc length. Auto-populated when [member tentacle_mesh] is a [code]TentacleMesh[/code] resource that exposes [code]get_baked_girth_texture[/code].

method.name: get_segment_stretch_ratios
method.description: Current segment length / rest length, one entry per segment. 1.0 = at rest, >1 stretched, <1 compressed. Used by the gizmo overlay to color-code stretch.

method.name: get_solver
method.description: Underlying [PBDSolver]. Direct manipulation is allowed for tests + tooling; the Tentacle's exports are forwarding setters onto the same instance.

method.name: get_spline_data_image
method.description: CPU-side mirror of the per-tick spline data texture (RGBA32F). For inspection / tests only — the GPU texture is what the shader actually reads.

method.name: get_spline_data_texture
method.description: Per-tick spline data texture (RGBA32F) bound to the shader as [code]spline_data_texture[/code]. Owned by the Tentacle, never reallocated after [code]_ready[/code].

method.name: get_spline_data_texture_width
method.description: Width of the spline data texture in samples. Bound to the shader as [code]spline_data_width[/code].

method.name: get_spline_frames
method.description: Sampled parallel-transport frames along the chain's centerline; an array of [code]count[/code] dictionaries with [code]tangent[/code] / [code]normal[/code] / [code]binormal[/code] vectors. For tooling / gizmos.

method.name: get_spline_samples
method.description: [code]count[/code] equally-spaced world-space points along the chain's spline.

method.name: get_target_pull_state
method.description: Snapshot of the single tip target-pull: [code]{ active: bool, particle_index: int, world_pos: Vector3, stiffness: float }[/code].

method.name: rebuild_chain
method.description: Reset the particle chain to its rest pose at the current node transform. Called automatically when [member particle_count] or [member segment_length] changes; expose for tooling that wants a hard reset.

method.name: set_anchor_transform
method.description: Override the anchor with an explicit world transform. Call [method clear_anchor_override] to restore the default (follow this node's [code]global_transform[/code]).

method.name: set_pose_targets
method.description: Soft per-particle pose pulls — the behavior driver writes these every tick to shape the chain. Three parallel arrays of equal length; cleared via [method clear_pose_targets].

method.name: set_rest_girth_texture
method.description: Override the rest-girth profile manually. Normally auto-populated from [member tentacle_mesh]; only call when wiring a stock primitive that doesn't expose a baked profile.

method.name: set_target
method.description: Set a single tip target-pull at [code]world_pos[/code] with the current [method get_target_stiffness].

method.name: set_target_particle_index
method.description: Which particle the single target-pull acts on. Defaults to the tip ([code]particle_count - 1[/code]).

method.name: set_target_stiffness
method.description: Per-iteration stiffness of the single tip target-pull. 0 = ignored, 1 = closes the gap each iteration. Compounds across iterations (4 iters × 0.2 ≈ 0.59 effective).

method.name: tick
method.description: Drive one solver step. Called automatically from [code]_physics_process[/code]; exposed so headless tests can drive the same pipeline without the SceneTree.

method.name: update_render_data
method.description: Force a refresh of the spline data texture from the current particle state. Called automatically at end-of-tick; exposed for tools that mutate solver state outside the tick (e.g. authoring gizmos).

members.member: How fast directional squeeze (orifice ring pressure, future) decays per second. Higher = snappier recovery; lower = longer-lived deformation.

Cap on the angular speed (rad/s) of the dynamic particle adjacent to the anchor as it sweeps around the anchor in one tick. 0 disables. Use to tame whip-back when pose pulls or inertia would otherwise spin the base too fast.

Static friction coefficient μ_s at contact (§4.4 smooth-vs-skin-dry default). Modulated by [member tentacle_lubricity] before being handed to the solver. Friction cancels tangential motion within the cone μ_s · Δn; outside it, motion is capped at the kinetic cone.

Per-iteration stiffness of the bending constraint (resists curvature, pushing the chain back toward straight). Compounds across iterations. 0 = noodle, 1 = stiff. Combine with [member distance_stiffness] to balance "muscle tone".

Multiplier on the friction reciprocal impulse applied to dynamic bodies (RigidBody3D / PhysicalBone3D / etc.) at contact. With slice 4G's physics-correct friction the spec's full impulse [code]J = μ_k × N × dt[/code] is what gets applied at scale=1.0; dial down for "tentacle barely moves the bones" or up for "tentacle is heavier than physics". Set to 0 to fully decouple ragdoll/dynamic-body reactions while keeping the chain itself responsive to contact.

Distance-constraint stiffness on segments where either endpoint is in active contact (§4.3 slice 4C). Below [member distance_stiffness] so the chain stretches temporarily over wrapped geometry instead of fighting collision push-out. Snaps back to rest length when contact ends.

Slice 4K — when true (default), in [code]predict()[/code] the gravity step [code]gravity × dt²[/code] is projected onto the contact tangent plane for particles whose probe reports an active contact, instead of being added in full. Physical model: the contact supports the particle's weight (a brick on a floor doesn't sink) — only tangent gravity (slope-driven sliding) acts. Eliminates the per-tick "gravity-sinks-particle / iter-pushes-out" cycle that seeds tick-rate jitter the iter loop amplifies in wedged configurations. Disable for legacy "gravity always pulls down regardless of contact" behavior.

Implicit-velocity damping for in-contact particles. Lerp factor (0..1) applied at end-of-tick to in-contact particles' [code]prev_position[/code] toward [code]position[/code], which bleeds the per-tick implicit velocity carried via Verlet integration. PBD's iterate loop can fail to converge when bending / pose / distance pull a contacting particle inward and collision pushes out — each iter adds net drift, accumulating into visible tick-rate jitter ("tentacle jitters between two colliders"). 0 = disabled, 0.5 (default) = halve velocity per tick, 1.0 = fully kill velocity for in-contact particles. Free particles unaffected; legitimate sliding (high tick-to-tick velocity) decays slowly compared to sub-millimeter oscillation, so cling-and-slide motion stays fluid.
Per-tick velocity retention. 1.0 = lossless, 0.0 = snaps to anchor every tick. Default 0.99 reads as "underwater drag"; drop toward 0.95 for a stiffer, more-damped feel.
Per-iteration stiffness of segment-length distance constraints. 1.0 = rigid; below 1.0 lets segments stretch under load. See [member contact_stiffness] for the during-contact override.
Spawn the runtime debug overlay (particles, constraints, environment probe, friction arrows) as a child node. The overlay reads snapshots every frame; cheap to leave on while iterating.

Physics-layer mask the per-particle sphere probe filters against. Use to exclude e.g. ragdoll bones from the collision pipeline (so the tentacle doesn't push back on the hero). Default −1 = all layers.

[b]Legacy[/b] (slice 4A): max raycast distance for the gravity-only probe. Unused since slice 4D switched to per-particle sphere queries — the probe radius is now [member particle_collision_radius]. Kept to avoid breaking existing scenes that set the export.
Toggle for the unified collision probe. Disables ALL collision detection — environment AND ragdoll — when off. Use [member environment_collision_layer_mask] for selective filtering.
Per-tentacle gravity vector. Independent of the global physics gravity so individual tentacles can hang in different directions (e.g. a ceiling-mounted vs floor-anchored tentacle).

PBD iterations per tick (clamped to [1, 6]). Higher = stiffer constraints (each runs more times per tick) and more stable contact, but linearly more cost. 4 is the default for a good cost/quality tradeoff.
μ_k / μ_s. 0.8 (default) matches the §4.3 "typical" ratio. Drops below 1 so once a particle slips it slides easier than it grips.
Which mesh-local axis is the spline arc-length axis. Default Z (matches §10.1 and [code]TentacleMesh[/code]); use Y for stock CylinderMesh primitives (pair with [member mesh_arc_offset] = height/2).
Additive offset on the chosen arc axis after the sign multiplier. Use to convert a centered mesh (axis ∈ [−h/2, h/2]) to the base-at-zero convention the shader expects (arc ∈ [0, h]).

Sign multiplier on the chosen arc axis. −1 maps a tip-at-negative mesh onto the shader's positive-arc convention. Auto-set when [member tentacle_mesh] is a [code]TentacleMesh[/code]; leave +1 for stock primitives.

Per-particle collision sphere radius (m). Effective radius is [code]particle_collision_radius × girth_scale[/code] — the chain's auto-baked girth profile already scales it along the body. The probe queries with a 5% bias so tangent contacts still detect.
Chain length in particles. More = smoother bend at higher cost (PBD per-particle work scales linearly). 12–24 is a good range.

Pin the first N particles to the anchor transform. 1 (default) = single-particle anchor. 2 fixes the segment 0→1 orientation so the base doesn't tilt under pose pulls. 3 also locks 1→2, etc. Local offsets are captured at the time the count changes.
Distance between adjacent particles at rest (m). Total chain length = [code]segment_length × (particle_count − 1)[/code]. Reset live without rebuilding.
Per-instance [ShaderMaterial] driving the spline-skinned mesh. Each tentacle owns its own; the [code].gdshader[/code] file is shared. Auto-created on [code]_ready[/code] if not assigned.

Slice 4M-pre.2. Multiplier on target-pull stiffness for in-contact particles — applied uniformly to both the singleton tip target ([method set_target]) and every distributed pose target. Below 1.0 lets the chain *give* to constraints instead of fighting them; fixes the "tentacle jitters between two colliders" failure mode where pose / tip pulls fight collision push-out at full strength. Composes multiplicatively with [member tension_taper_threshold]'s ramp. Forwarded to the underlying [PBDSolver].

Slice 4T pose-target rate cap (m/s). Caps how fast the driver-supplied target position can move per outer Tentacle tick; the solver linearly interpolates toward the driver's intent so high-frequency target writes (an aggressive behaviour driver, a step-function target jump) don't shock the chain. Source-side complement to [member tension_taper_threshold] — the taper caps tension at the friction cone, this caps it at the source. Default 5.0 leaves headroom for probing mood's 1.4 m/s peak; aggressive moods can lower for slower / calmer motion; 0 disables the cap entirely. Cold-start (first call after [method set_target] / [method clear_target]) bypasses the cap so initial settling isn't artificially slow. Forwarded to the underlying [PBDSolver].

Slice 4Q-fix tension-aware target softening engagement threshold. When an in-contact particle's dominant contact slot has tangent_lambda exceeding [code]threshold × static_cone[/code] (where [code]static_cone = mu_s × normal_lambda[/code]), the target stiffness is further scaled linearly to zero at saturation ([code]tangent_lambda == static_cone[/code]). Composes multiplicatively with [member target_softness_when_blocked]. Default 0.8 — chain stops adding tension the static-friction cone can't hold, eliminating the build-then-slip stick-slip cycle observed under probing + low-lubricity contact. 1.0 disables (taper never engages); 0.0 always-engaged. Forwarded to the underlying [PBDSolver].

0..1 modulator on [member base_static_friction]. Scales μ_s as [code](1 − lubricity)[/code]. 1.0 = frictionless (slick); 0 = full base coefficient. The cleanest knob for "this tentacle is slippery / dry".

Mesh deformed along the chain. Accepts any [Mesh]; when assigned a [code]TentacleMesh[/code] resource, auto-detects baked girth, arc axis sign, and rest length. For stock primitives (CylinderMesh etc.), set [member mesh_arc_axis] / [member mesh_arc_sign] / [member mesh_arc_offset] manually.

constants.constant: Spline arc runs along the mesh's local +X.
Spline arc runs along the mesh's local +Y. Use for stock [CylinderMesh] (paired with [member mesh_arc_offset] = height/2).
Spline arc runs along the mesh's local +Z. Default; matches [code]TentacleMesh[/code].
